comment_text.sn9.5:1.2: The word “persuasion” (saññatti) is used in the context of discussions with skeptical non-Buddhists (eg. MN 60:8.5).
comment_text.sn9.5:1.3: The same verse is spoken by a certain Vajjiputta (“Vajjian”) at Thag 1.119. The Pali commentary says this occurred shortly after the Buddha’s passing. Ānanda, still only a stream-enterer, was striving for arahantship before the First Council, but got caught up in consoling the distraught layfolk. The Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinaya agrees, adding that Vajjiputta was Ānanda’s attendant at the time. Following the exhortation, Ānanda meditated till late at night, then mindfully lay down to rest. When his feet were lifted off the floor, but his head not yet on his pillow, he became awakened (Rockhill, The Life of the Buddha, p. 155). The Pali Vinaya recounts the same events, but does not mention this verse (Kd 21:1.6.1).
comment_text.sn9.5:2.2: Both Bodhi here and Norman in the parallel in Elders Verses (Thag 1.119:1.2) read osiya (“having laid”), which agrees with the commentarial gloss.

root_text.sn9.5:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ānando kosalesu viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.sn9.5:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā ānando ativelaṁ gihisaññattibahulo viharati.
root_text.sn9.5:1.3: Atha kho yā tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe adhivatthā devatā āyasmato ānandassa anukampikā atthakāmā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ saṁvejetukāmā yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn9.5:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando tāya devatāya saṁvejito saṁvegamāpādīti.

translation_text.sn9.5:1.1: At one time Venerable Ānanda was staying in the land of the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
translation_text.sn9.5:1.2: Now at that time Ānanda was spending too much time persuading lay people.
translation_text.sn9.5:1.3: Then the deity haunting that forest had sympathy for Ānanda, wanting what’s best for him. So they approached him wanting to stir him up, and recited these verses:
translation_text.sn9.5:2.1: “You’ve left for the jungle, the root of a tree,
translation_text.sn9.5:2.2: having laid extinguishment in your heart.
translation_text.sn9.5:2.4: What use is this hullabaloo to you?”
translation_text.sn9.5:3.1: Impelled by that deity, Venerable Ānanda was struck with a sense of urgency.

variant_text.sn9.5:2.3: gotama mā pamādo → mā ca pamādo (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed)
